mission.title: Install and Configure Kubefleet on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeFleet is an open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster application management solution that simplifies governance and workload management across multiple clusters. Installing KubeFleet allows you to efficiently manage applications in a hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

steps.title: Add the KubeFleet Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KubeFleet Helm repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kubefleet https://charts.kubefleet.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the KubeFleet hub agent
steps.description:
Install the KubeFleet hub agent using Helm, specifying the namespace and version:
```bash
helm install hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --create-namespace --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the hub agent pod is running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fleet-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for the hub agent
steps.description:
Update the resource limits for the hub agent by editing the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for KubeFleet
steps.description:
Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding for the KubeFleet hub agent:
```bash
kubectl create clusterrole hub-agent-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,patch,delete --resource=pods,services,deployments
kubectl create clusterrolebinding hub-agent-role-binding --clusterrole=hub-agent-role --serviceaccount=fleet-system:default
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeFleet will show the hub agent pod running in the 'fleet-system' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for the hub agent.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubefleet https://charts.kubefleet.dev
helm repo update
helm upgrade hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi

kubectl create clusterrole hub-agent-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,patch,delete --resource=pods,services,deployments
kubectl create clusterrolebinding hub-agent-role-binding --clusterrole=hub-agent-role --serviceaccount=fleet-system:default

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
